seo.title: AI eDiscovery Software | eDiscovery AI for Semantic Document Search
seo.description: AI eDiscovery software with semantic search across thousands of documents. eDiscovery AI that finds relevant evidence by meaning, not keywords. Starting at $30/mo.

hero.headline: AI eDiscovery: Powerful Document Search
hero.subheadline: Dodonai's eDiscovery AI searches thousands of documents by meaning, not keywords. Describe what you're looking for in plain language and get relevant results in seconds. Starting at $30/mo, it's enterprise-grade eDiscovery without the enterprise price tag.

educationalContent.title: What is eDiscovery AI Software?
educationalContent.description: eDiscovery AI software combines artificial intelligence with electronic discovery, the process lawyers use to collect and analyze digital data in legal cases. It sorts and organizes emails, documents, and social media records, uses semantic search that understands meaning rather than just keywords, and improves through continuous learning. eDiscovery AI makes sifting through digital evidence faster, more accurate, and far easier than legacy keyword-based platforms.

educationalContent.title: Semantic Search vs. Keyword Search
educationalContent.description: Traditional eDiscovery tools rely on keyword matching. You type a term and the software returns documents containing that exact word. This approach misses relevant documents that use synonyms or describe the same concept in different language. Dodonai's AI eDiscovery understands the meaning behind your query: ask for documents about 'breach of contract disputes' and the system finds relevant evidence even when that exact phrase never appears. The result is fewer false positives and fewer missed documents.

educationalContent.title: How Lawyers Use AI eDiscovery in Litigation
educationalContent.description: Litigation teams use AI eDiscovery to search and review thousands of documents in a fraction of the time manual review takes. Common workflows include identifying hot documents during early case assessment, running privilege reviews, and building chronologies from email chains. Dodonai's eDiscovery AI integrates with {{link:/deposition-summary-software/|deposition summary}} and {{link:/ai-medical-record-summaries-chronologies/|medical record summary}} workflows, giving teams a single platform for the major document-intensive tasks in litigation.

educationalContent.title: AI eDiscovery vs. Legacy Platform Costs
educationalContent.description: Legacy eDiscovery platforms like Relativity and Everlaw charge per-gigabyte hosting fees, per-user licensing, and processing surcharges that can total $50,000+ per year for mid-size firms. Dodonai's AI eDiscovery provides semantic search, document review, and {{link:/ai-pdf-ocr/|OCR processing}} starting at $30/mo. That makes enterprise-grade eDiscovery accessible to solo practitioners and small firms without enterprise budgets. See our {{link:/compare/ediscovery-software-review/|eDiscovery software comparison}} for a detailed breakdown.

aiOverview.heading: Concept-Based Discovery: Search by Meaning, Not Just Keywords
paragraphs: Dodonai's eDiscovery AI goes beyond keyword matching to deliver concept-based document discovery. Describe what you're looking for in plain language and the system finds relevant documents even when they use completely different terminology. No more running dozens of keyword variations to make sure you haven't missed something.

Every search result shows you exactly which passages matched and why, so you can confirm relevance and document your review methodology for the court. Combined with automated privilege detection and structured data extraction, Dodonai's AI eDiscovery gives litigation teams a review process that's both thorough and defensible.

howItWorks.title: Upload your Documentary Evidence
howItWorks.description: Upload your PDFs, TXT, or Word documents (or a ZIP of them) to Dodonai. For larger collections, our team can handle the upload for you.

howItWorks.title: Search Semantically
howItWorks.description: Skip the keyword guessing game. Describe the type of information you want to find in plain language. Dodonai's eDiscovery AI searches your documents for matching concepts, not exact words.

howItWorks.title: See Results
howItWorks.description: Dodonai returns ranked snippets of your documentary evidence, with the most relevant passages surfaced first. Click through to the source document to verify.

faq.question: What is AI eDiscovery?
faq.answer: AI eDiscovery (also called eDiscovery AI) uses artificial intelligence to automate the searching, sorting, and analysis of digital data in litigation. Instead of manually reviewing thousands of documents, legal teams can search by concept and let the AI surface relevant evidence across large document collections.

faq.question: How does AI improve the eDiscovery process?
faq.answer: AI speeds up eDiscovery by analyzing large volumes of documents in seconds instead of days. Semantic search finds relevant evidence even when documents use different terminology than your search terms, which cuts false negatives. And automated classification (privilege, relevance, responsiveness) reduces the hours spent on manual review.

faq.question: Can AI eDiscovery software replace lawyers or paralegals?
faq.answer: No. AI eDiscovery software handles the time-consuming parts (searching, sorting, initial classification) so lawyers and paralegals can focus on the judgment calls: case strategy, privilege determinations, and building arguments. The AI surfaces what's relevant; the attorney decides what to do with it.

faq.question: Is AI eDiscovery secure and confidential?
faq.answer: Yes. Dodonai is SOC 2 certified and HIPAA compliant, with AES-256 encryption in transit and at rest. We don't train AI models on your data, and our LLM providers operate under zero data retention (ZDR) policies. You control when your data is deleted.

faq.question: How does Dodonai's AI eDiscovery differ from other eDiscovery tools?
faq.answer: 3 things: (1) semantic search that finds documents by meaning, not keywords, (2) pricing that starts at $30/mo instead of $50,000+/year, and (3) zero setup time (upload and search in minutes, no implementation project). For a detailed comparison, see our {{link:/compare/ediscovery-software-review/|eDiscovery software comparison}}.

faq.question: Is Dodonai's AI eDiscovery software suitable for all sizes of law firms?
faq.answer: Yes. Dodonai's AI eDiscovery starts at $30/mo, which makes it practical for solo practitioners and small firms. But the same semantic search and document review tools scale to handle the volume a larger firm would throw at it. You're not locked into a tier.

faq.question: What types of documents can I search with eDiscovery AI?
faq.answer: PDFs, Word documents, emails, text files, and scanned documents. For scanned or image-based documents, Dodonai's {{link:/ai-pdf-ocr/|AI OCR}} converts them to searchable text before indexing. So even paper-based document productions become fully searchable through eDiscovery AI.

faq.question: How much does AI eDiscovery cost compared to Relativity or Everlaw?
faq.answer: Legacy platforms like Relativity and Everlaw charge per-gigabyte hosting fees plus per-user licensing, easily totaling $50,000+/year for a mid-size firm. Dodonai's AI eDiscovery starts at $30/mo with no per-gigabyte surcharges. See our {{link:/pricing/|pricing page}} for full details or read our {{link:/compare/ediscovery-software-review/|eDiscovery software comparison}}.

faq.question: Can I use eDiscovery AI with scanned or image-based PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. Dodonai's {{link:/ai-pdf-ocr/|AI-powered OCR}} processes scanned documents, faxes, and image-based PDFs automatically. Once OCR is complete, the extracted text is indexed and fully searchable using semantic search. No manual preprocessing required.

faq.question: What is eDiscovery Software?
faq.answer: eDiscovery (electronic discovery) is the process of identifying, collecting, and producing electronically stored information (ESI) as evidence in legal cases. ESI includes emails, documents, databases, voicemail, social media, and more. The process has several stages: identification, preservation, collection, processing, review, and production. As digital data volumes have grown, eDiscovery software has become essential for litigation, investigations, and FOIA requests.

faq.question: I have more questions. Where can I find answers?
faq.answer: Visit our {{link:https://app.dodon.ai/help|Help Center}} for detailed guides on uploading and searching documents. You can also contact us at hello@dodon.ai with any questions.
